senses.definition: feeling irritated or slightly angry

examples.x: She felt annoyed when her computer froze in the middle of her work.
The annoyed customer complained about the long wait at the restaurant.
She felt annoyed when someone repeatedly interrupted her during the meeting.
He became annoyed when his phone kept buzzing with notifications.
The annoyed expression on her face showed her frustration with the slow internet connection.

senses.definition: (of devices or processes) being or working with little or no human involvement

examples.x: The garage door has an automatic opener, so you can open it with just the push of a button.
The washing machine has an automatic cycle that starts and stops on its own, making laundry chores easier.
Many cars now come equipped with automatic braking systems that help prevent accidents.
The coffee maker has an automatic timer, so you can wake up to freshly brewed coffee in the morning.
The factory has installed automatic conveyor belts to move products efficiently along the assembly line.

senses.definition: having an understanding or perception of something, often through careful thought or sensitivity

examples.x: She became aware of the time when she heard the clock chiming.
The company made employees aware of the new policies through email notifications.
He wasn't aware of the consequences of his actions until it was too late.
The students were not aware of the upcoming test until the teacher reminded them.
She became aware of her surroundings as she walked through the unfamiliar neighborhood.

senses.definition: related or belonging to people, not machines or animals

examples.x: The human brain is capable of remarkable feats of creativity and innovation.
Despite advances in technology, human interaction remains a crucial aspect of our daily lives.
Researchers are constantly studying human behavior to better understand what drives our decisions and actions.
Compassion and empathy are inherently human qualities that help us connect with one another.

senses.definition: forming or being the necessary part of something, on which other things are built

examples.x: Learning basic arithmetic skills is essential for understanding more advanced math concepts.
He taught her the basic rules of chess before they started playing.
The basic ingredients for the recipe include flour, sugar, and eggs.
Understanding basic grammar rules is important for writing clear and effective sentences.

senses.definition: located at or near the center or middle of something

examples.x: The hotel is located in a central part of the city, close to all the major attractions.
The central bus station is just a few blocks away from the shopping district.
They decided to open their new office in a central business district for better accessibility.
The park is situated in a central area of town, making it convenient for residents to visit.
Living in a central neighborhood allows easy access to schools, hospitals, and supermarkets.

senses.definition: having or made of several parts

examples.x: The human brain is a complex organ with billions of interconnected neurons.
The novel's plot is complex, with multiple intertwined storylines.
The project required a complex strategy involving various departments.
He designed a complex machine that performs multiple functions simultaneously.
The complex design of the machine required careful assembly.

senses.definition: feeling uncertain or not confident about something because it is not clear or easy to understand

examples.x: She felt confused after reading the complicated instructions.
He seemed confused about which direction to take in his career.
The students looked confused as they struggled to grasp the complex concept.
She felt confused about her feelings towards her friend.
The instructions were so unclear that they left everyone feeling confused.

senses.definition: favorable or well-suited for a specific purpose or situation

examples.x: The meeting time was convenient for everyone involved.
This route is convenient if you're traveling to the airport.
It was convenient that the train arrived just as I reached the station.
I love that the library has extended hours; it’s very convenient for students.
The flexible hours at the clinic are very convenient for my schedule.

senses.definition: involving a society's customs, traditions, beliefs, and other related matters

examples.x: The museum features exhibits showcasing various cultural artifacts from around the world.
Language is an integral part of cultural identity, reflecting shared values and traditions.
The festival celebrates the rich cultural diversity of the community through music, dance, and food.
Cultural differences can lead to misunderstandings, but they also offer opportunities for learning and growth.
The anthropologist studied the cultural practices of the indigenous tribe living in the remote region.

senses.definition: happening or existing in the present time

examples.x: The current economic conditions are challenging for many businesses.
Please check the current availability of the product on the website.
The current weather forecast predicts sunny skies for the weekend.
The current population of the city is estimated to be around one million.
She is studying the current trends in fashion for her design project.
The current negotiations between the two companies are expected to lead to a significant partnership.
The team is working on current projects that aim to revolutionize the industry's approach to sustainability.

senses.definition: not satisfied or happy with something, because it did not meet one's expectations or hopes

examples.x: She felt disappointed when her favorite team lost the championship game.
His parents were clearly disappointed that he didn’t pass the exam.
The disappointed expression on her face revealed her sadness.
The coach seemed disappointed with the team’s performance.

senses.definition: having had too much alcohol and visibly affected by it

examples.x: The bar refused to serve more drinks to visibly drunk patrons.
Being drunk can lead to health risks and accidents.
She regretted the decisions made while drunk the previous night.
The legal limit for driving is lower for individuals who are drunk.
He became drunk after consuming several glasses of wine at the party.

senses.definition: situated in the east

examples.x: The sun rises in the eastern part of the sky.
Our house is located on the eastern side of the street.
The city's eastern districts are known for their vibrant cultural diversity.
The eastern border of the country is marked by a river.
They traveled to the eastern region of the country for their vacation.
The house has a beautiful view of the eastern mountains.

senses.definition: causing a person to feel ashamed or uneasy

examples.x: His embarrassing slip on the banana peel in front of everyone made him blush with embarrassment.
Forgetting someone's name in a crowded room can be embarrassing.
Accidentally sending a private text message to the wrong person is always embarrassing.
Being caught in a lie can lead to an embarrassing situation.
His embarrassing behavior at the dinner table made the guests uncomfortable.

senses.definition: having the same amount, size, quantity, etc.

examples.x: The twins received equal portions of cake for their birthdays.
Sarah and David each received an equal share of the profits from their business venture.
Both teams scored an equal number of goals, resulting in a tie game.
All students in the class were given equal time to present their projects.
The company prides itself on providing equal pay for equal work to all employees.

senses.definition: very large in size

examples.x: The huge skyscraper dominated the city skyline.
He bought a huge television that took up an entire wall in his living room.
She adopted a huge dog that was almost as big as she was.
The huge tree in their backyard provided shade for the entire garden.
They built a huge sandcastle that towered over the other ones on the beach.

senses.definition: very necessary for a particular purpose or situation

examples.x: Adequate nutrition is essential for overall health and well-being.
Sunscreen is essential for protecting your skin from harmful UV rays.
Regular exercise is essential for maintaining physical fitness and health.
Communication is essential for building strong relationships.
Safety equipment is essential for workers in hazardous environments.

senses.definition: easily recognized due to prior contact or involvement, often evoking a sense of comfort or ease

examples.x: The old house had a familiar smell that reminded her of childhood.
She felt a sense of comfort in the familiar surroundings of her hometown.
The song had a familiar melody that brought back memories of happier times.
The professor's teaching style was familiar to the students who had taken his class before.
I found the street name familiar, as I had walked past it before.

senses.definition: unable to be moved or changed physically

examples.x: The mechanic tightened the fixed bolts to ensure the machine's stability.

senses.definition: regarding or affecting the entire world

examples.x: The pandemic has had a global impact on public health, economies, and daily life.
Climate change is a global issue that requires collective action from nations around the world.
The company operates on a global scale, with offices and operations in multiple countries.
The United Nations works to address global challenges such as poverty, hunger, and human rights violations.
The internet enables global communication and access to information across continents.

senses.definition: belonging to or significant in the past

examples.x: They visited a historical battlefield to learn more about the country's heritage.
The museum exhibits many historical artifacts from different eras.
The town is known for its historical monuments and ancient architecture.
The historical mansion is now a museum open to the public.
The documentary explored a major historical event.

senses.definition: not real and existing only in the mind rather than in physical reality

examples.x: The unicorn in the story was purely imaginary, a creature of fantasy and folklore.
The imaginary friend that the child spoke to was a creation of their imagination, not a real person.
She often retreated into an imaginary world when feeling stressed, where she could escape reality.
The conspiracy theory was built upon imaginary connections and speculations, lacking any factual basis.

senses.definition: (of a place, space, etc.) situated inside a building, house, etc.

examples.x: The indoor pool at the gym provides a convenient option for swimming regardless of the weather outside.
Indoor basketball courts offer a controlled environment for players to practice and compete.
Indoor rock climbing gyms simulate natural rock formations for climbers to practice their skills.
The indoor skating rink is a popular destination for families to enjoy ice skating during the winter months.

senses.definition: physically harmed or wounded

examples.x: The injured athlete was taken off the field on a stretcher after a collision with another player.
Mary's injured arm was placed in a sling to immobilize it while it healed.
Jack's injured hand was wrapped in bandages to protect the cuts and bruises.

senses.definition: not having committed a wrongdoing or offense

examples.x: The evidence presented in court proved that he was innocent of the crime he was accused of.
The innocent bystander witnessed the accident and provided crucial testimony to the police.
The jury found the defendant innocent of all charges due to lack of evidence.
The innocent employee was cleared of suspicion after a thorough investigation revealed their innocence.
The innocent driver was not at fault for the car accident caused by the other driver's negligence.

senses.definition: authorized according to the law and official regulations

examples.x: The court ruled that the search conducted by law enforcement was legal.
The contract was deemed legal as it complied with all terms and conditions outlined in the official rules set forth by the regulatory authority.
The attorney advised the client to proceed with the transaction, assuring them that it was legal.
The judge dismissed the case, confirming that the defendant's actions were legal within the state's official rules.

senses.definition: describing or practicing special abilities or powers

examples.x: She possessed magic powers that allowed her to heal wounds instantly.
The ancient artifact was said to have magic abilities to control the weather.
The wizard's cloak had magic properties that made him invisible to others.

senses.definition: describing the people who have lived in an area for a very long time

examples.x: The native peoples of the Arctic, such as the Inuit and Yupik, have adapted to the harsh conditions of the region and have sustained themselves through hunting, fishing, and traditional practices for millennia.
The native tribes of the Amazon rainforest have inhabited the region for countless generations, living in harmony with nature.

senses.definition: positioned in the direction of the north

examples.x: The northern star guides travelers in the night sky.
The northern region of the country experiences harsh winters.
Our cabin is situated in the northern part of the forest.
The northern border of the state is marked by a river.
Northern cities often experience colder temperatures and shorter daylight hours in winter.

senses.definition: belonging to or following the methods or thoughts that are old as opposed to new or different ones

examples.x: The restaurant offers a traditional menu, focusing on familiar comfort foods rather than trendy dishes.
He has a traditional approach to parenting, believing in firm rules and routines.
She decorated her living room in a traditional style, with classic wooden furniture and antique lamps.
They decorated the house with traditional holiday ornaments.
The company’s traditional dress code requires formal attire, while other workplaces are adopting casual policies.

senses.definition: related or belonging to a period of history that is long gone
not seen by or unknown to other people

examples.x: We held a secret meeting to discuss the company's future plans.
There's a secret garden hidden behind the old house.
They had a secret meeting to discuss the surprise party.
The team worked on a secret project that no one outside the company knew about.

senses.definition: having great importance

examples.x: The CEO’s resignation was a big event that shook the entire company.
The environmental impact of the project is a big concern for the community.
The company's board faced a big decision regarding the future of the organization.
The conference was a big opportunity for networking with industry leaders.

senses.definition: unable to be easily seen or found
